title: carpinus macrostachya
intro: Carpinus macrostachya, also known as the large-bracted hornbeam, is a deciduous tree native to China. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Carpinus macrostachya are said to include reducing fever, relieving rheumatism, and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the tree are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, although more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these preparations.
